cards.label: 01 · notion
cards.title: Notion AI 3 = workspace as the agent.
cards.body: Notion AI now talks to your databases, your pages, your Slack, your Gmail — all from one chat box inside Notion. It plans, drafts, updates pages, sends DMs. Workspace-native agent.

cards.label: 02 · claude
cards.title: Claude Connectors = MCP, but consumer-ready.
cards.body: Connectors let Claude (web + desktop + mobile) talk to Google Drive, Slack, Notion, Linear, Gmail — without you running an MCP server. One-click OAuth, zero infra. MCP under the hood.

cards.label: 03 · chatgpt
cards.title: ChatGPT Apps = mini-apps inside chat.
cards.body: Third parties (Spotify, Canva, Zillow, Booking) ship interactive UIs that render inside the ChatGPT chat. Pick a song, browse a listing, design a card — without leaving the conversation.
cards.metric: → free on ChatGPT · launching in waves

cards.label: 04 · pick
cards.title: When to pick which.
cards.body: Lives in Notion daily → Notion AI 3. Wants Claude smart + tools-everywhere → Connectors. Lives in ChatGPT + wants third-party UI → Apps. The three are converging — same idea, different surface.

cards.label: 05 · build
cards.title: Want to build for these?
cards.body: All three speak MCP. Build one MCP server, expose it as a Claude Connector + a ChatGPT App + a Notion integration. One protocol, three distribution channels. Massive leverage.

take.use_if: You're picking which AI surface to invest in — pick the one that matches where your actual work happens.
take.skip_if: You jump between AIs constantly — none of these will save you, you need a meta-layer (which is the StackPicks Connect bet).
